---
title: "Expo app"
description: "Build an Expo web app template with Node.js."
---

# Expo app

This example bootstraps an Expo app during the template build and starts the development server when the sandbox boots.

## Template definition

```go
package main

import e2b "github.com/superduck-ai/e2b-go-sdk"

var template = e2b.Template(nil).
	FromNodeImage().
	SetWorkdir("/home/user/expo-app").
	RunCmd("npx create-expo-app@latest . --yes").
	RunCmd("mv /home/user/expo-app/* /home/user/ && rm -rf /home/user/expo-app").
	SetWorkdir("/home/user").
	SetStartCmd("npx expo start --web --port 8081", e2b.WaitForURL("http://127.0.0.1:8081"))
```

## Build

```go
package main

import (
	"context"
	"log"

	e2b "github.com/superduck-ai/e2b-go-sdk"
)

func main() {
	buildInfo, err := e2b.Build(context.Background(), template, "expo-app", &e2b.BuildOptions{
		BasicBuildOptions: e2b.BasicBuildOptions{
			CpuCount:    4,
			MemoryMB:    8192,
			OnBuildLogs: e2b.DefaultBuildLogger(),
		},
	})
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}

	log.Println(buildInfo.TemplateID)
}
```

## Create a sandbox and open the app

```go
package main

import (
	"context"
	"log"

	e2b "github.com/superduck-ai/e2b-go-sdk"
)

func main() {
	sandbox, err := e2b.Create(context.Background(), "expo-app", nil)
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	defer sandbox.Kill(context.Background(), nil)

	log.Println("Expo URL:", "https://"+sandbox.GetHost(8081))
}
```
